name: SvgParser

unions.name: SvgNode
unions.comment: A SVG node can be one of the three: SvgElement, SvgText or SvgComment.

aliases.name: Element
aliases.comment:
An Element consists of a tag name, a list of attributes, a list of children nodes.

    <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"></svg>

will be parsed as

    Element "svg" [("xmlns", "http://www.w3.org/2000/svg")] []
aliases.type: { name : String, attributes : List SvgAttribute, children : List SvgNode }

aliases.name: SvgAttribute
aliases.comment: A name/value pair to denote attribute.

values.name: nodeToSvg
values.comment: Convert `SvgNode` to `Svg msg`. This is useful when you want to manipulate `SvgNode` before conveting to `Html msg`.

values.name: parse
values.comment: Parses `String` to `Html msg`. Usually this is the only function you need.

values.name: parseToNode
values.comment:
Parses `String` to `SvgNode`. Normally you will use `parse` instead of this.

    parse "<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\"></svg>"
        == Ok (SvgElement (Element "svg" [("xmlns", "http://www.w3.org/2000/svg")] []))

values.name: toAttribute
values.comment: Convert `SvgAttribute` to `Attribute msg`. This is useful when you want to manipulate `SvgAttribute` before converting to `Html msg`.
